name: Jameis Winston
headline: Backup arm behind Dart with no standalone value
body:
Winston enters 2026 locked into a reserve role behind Jaxson Dart in New York, and his recent profile underscores why the ceiling stays capped. His three-game 2025 sample was loud but unstable: a 0.259 EPA per play that outran a 43.4% success rate, a minus-3.1 CPOE, and a 25.8% bad-throw rate. The bigger 2024 body of work in Cleveland tells the truer story, eleven starts producing 13 touchdowns against 12 picks on a flat minus-0.044 EPA and a sub-44% success rate.

That boom-bust volatility, paired with backup snaps, leaves nothing to draft in standard formats. Projections peg him near 154 pass yards a game with one score and one interception, a profile worth only a last-round Superflex flier if you already own Dart. Everywhere else he sits on waivers, a name to monitor only if injury hands him the job and the easy schedule behind it.
